```{r}
library(Signac)
library(Seurat)
library(GenomeInfoDb)
library(TFBSTools)
library(JASPAR2022) # or JASPAR2024 if available
library(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38) # change genome as needed
library(rtracklayer)
library(EnsDb.Hsapiens.v86)
library(motifmatchr)
library(chromVAR)
library(BiocParallel)
library(SeuratDisk)
```

```{r}
library(JASPAR2022)
library(Matrix)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
```


```{r}
# paths
fragpath <- "HUA8138A3/outs/fragments.tsv.gz"

# 1) peaks
peaks <- rtracklayer::import("HUA8138A3/outs/peaks.bed")
peaks <- keepStandardChromosomes(peaks, pruning.mode = "coarse")
peaks <- sort(peaks)

# 2) fragments (ensure the .tbi exists next to the .gz)
frags <- CreateFragmentObject(
  path = fragpath
  # , cells = readLines("HUA8138A4/outs/filtered_peak_bc_matrix/barcodes.tsv.gz") # optional filter
)

# 3) counts matrix over peaks
counts <- FeatureMatrix(
  fragments = frags,
  features  = peaks
)

# 4) chromatin assay
atac <- CreateChromatinAssay(
  counts    = counts,
  sep       = c(":", "-"),
  genome    = "hg38",
  fragments = frags
)

# 5) Seurat object
obj <- CreateSeuratObject(
  counts = atac,
  assay  = "ATAC"
)

DefaultAssay(obj) <- "ATAC"
```

```{r}
# Gene annotation (for TSS, gene annotations)
annotation <- GetGRangesFromEnsDb(EnsDb.Hsapiens.v86)
seqlevelsStyle(annotation) <- "UCSC"   # e.g., "chr1"
genome(annotation) <- "hg38"
Annotation(obj) <- annotation

# TSS enrichment & nucleosome signal
obj <- TSSEnrichment(obj, fast = TRUE)
obj <- NucleosomeSignal(obj)

# Fraction of reads in peaks (FRiP) & blacklist ratio if you have a blacklist GRanges
#obj$pct_reads_in_peaks <- obj$peak_region_fragments / #obj$nCount_ATAC * 100

```

```{r}
#Optional frag_stats QC

frag_stats <- Signac::CountFragments(fragments = frags)

#Normalize column names if needed (handles version/name differences)
cn <- colnames(frag_stats)
if ("reads_in_peaks" %in% cn && !"peak_region_fragments" %in% cn) {
  frag_stats$peak_region_fragments <- frag_stats$reads_in_peaks
}
if ("fragments" %in% cn && !"passed_filters" %in% cn) {
  frag_stats$passed_filters <- frag_stats$fragments
}

#Keep only cells present in your object, and order rows to match colnames(obj)
frag_stats <- frag_stats[frag_stats$barcode %in% colnames(obj), ]
frag_stats <- frag_stats[match(colnames(obj), frag_stats$barcode), ]

#Add to metadata
rownames(frag_stats) <- frag_stats$barcode
to_add <- frag_stats[, intersect(colnames(frag_stats),
                                 c("passed_filters","peak_region_fragments","blacklist_fraction"))]
obj <- AddMetaData(obj, metadata = to_add)
```

```{r}
# Quick QC filters
obj <- subset(
  obj,
  subset =
    nCount_ATAC > 3000 &
    nCount_ATAC < 100000 &
    #pct_reads_in_peaks > 15 &
    TSS.enrichment > 2 &
    nucleosome_signal < 4
)
```


```{r}
# 6) Dimensionality reduction (TF-IDF + LSI)

obj <- RunTFIDF(obj)
obj <- FindTopFeatures(obj, min.cutoff = "q0") # keep all peaks for LSI
obj <- RunSVD(obj, reduction.key = "LSI_", reduction.name = "lsi")

# Neighborhood graph, UMAP, clusters
obj <- RunUMAP(obj, reduction = "lsi", dims = 2:30)
obj <- FindNeighbors(obj, reduction = "lsi", dims = 2:30)
obj <- FindClusters(obj, resolution = 0.5)

DimPlot(obj, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_clusters")
```

```{r}
obj <- FindNeighbors(obj, reduction = "lsi", dims = 2:30, graph.name = "lsi_snn")
obj <- FindClusters(obj, graph.name = 'lsi_snn', resolution = 0.2) # Adjust resolution as needed
```

```{r}
DimPlot(obj, reduction = "lsi", group.by = "seurat_clusters", dims = c(2, 3)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#279e68", "#d62728", "#aa40fc"))
```

```{r}
pdf("LSI_SALL2KO_5clusters.pdf", width = 6, height = 6)
DimPlot(obj, reduction = "lsi", group.by = "seurat_clusters", dims = c(2, 3)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#279e68", "#d62728", "#aa40fc"))
dev.off()
```

```{r}
FeaturePlot(obj, features = "TSS.percentile", reduction = "lsi")
```

```{r}
# 7) Add motifs to peaks

pfm <- getMatrixSet(
  x = JASPAR2022,
  opts = list(species = 9606, collection = "CORE")
)

obj <- AddMotifs(
  object = obj,
  genome = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38,
  pfm    = pfm
)
```

```{r}
# 8) Run chromVAR

# (chromVAR will compute GC bias, background peaks, then deviations)

register(SerialParam())

obj <- RunChromVAR(
  object = obj,
  genome = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38
)

DefaultAssay(obj) <- "chromvar"
```

```{r}
saveRDS(obj, file = "atac_chromvar_SALL2KO.rds")
```

```{r}
# Test plot by using assigned motif names
FeaturePlot(obj, features = c("MA0490.2"), min.cutoff = "q10", dims = c(2,3), reduction = 'lsi')
```

```{r}
# Differential motif activity between clusters (e.g., cluster 0 vs all)
DefaultAssay(obj) <- "chromvar"
Idents(obj) <- "seurat_clusters"

Cluster <- "4"
markers_motifs <- FindMarkers(
  object = obj,
  ident.1 = Cluster,
  only.pos = TRUE,
  assay   = "chromvar",
  test.use = "wilcox"
)
head(markers_motifs)
```

```{r}
write.csv(markers_motifs, paste0("cluster",Cluster,"_motifs_5clusters.csv"), row.names = TRUE)
```


```{r}
# 9) Explore TF motif activity (chromVAR deviations)


# Heatmap of selected motif activities (choose a set of motifs)
DefaultAssay(obj) <- "chromvar"
motifs_to_plot <- rownames(markers_motifs)[1:20]
DoHeatmap(obj, features = motifs_to_plot, group.by = "seurat_clusters")
```


```{r}
obj <- readRDS("atac_chromvar_SALL2KO.rds")
```

```{r}
dim(Embeddings(obj, "lsi"))
```

```{r}
#Optional: convert object to h5ad for analysis in Scanpy

obj <- DietSeurat(
  obj,
  assays     = c("ATAC", "chromvar"),   
  dimreducs  = c("lsi", "umap"),
  counts     = TRUE,
  data       = TRUE,
  scale.data = FALSE,
  graphs     = NULL
)

SaveH5Seurat(obj, filename = "atac_chromvar_SALL2KO.h5Seurat")
Convert("atac_chromvar_SALL2KO.h5Seurat", dest = "h5ad")
```


```{r}
# Get a tf_name to motif match file

motifs <- getMatrixSet(JASPAR2022, opts = list(species = 9606, collection = "CORE"))

# Convert to data frame
motif_meta <- as.data.frame(do.call(rbind, lapply(motifs, function(m) {
  data.frame(
    motif_id = ID(m),
    tf_name  = name(m),
    family   = m@tags$family,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})))
head(motif_meta)

write.csv(motif_meta, paste0("tf_name_to_motif.csv"), row.names = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#Experimental: Count normalized motif intensity

# 1) get the binary peak×motif match matrix
motif_mat <- GetMotifData(obj, assay = "ATAC", slot = "data")  # peaks x motifs

# 2) find peaks that contain the motif (ID = MA0490.2)
motif_id <- "MA0143.4"
stopifnot(motif_id %in% colnames(motif_mat))
peaks_with_motif <- rownames(motif_mat)[motif_mat[, motif_id] > 0]

# 3) extract counts for those peaks and compute per-cell sums/fractions
DefaultAssay(obj) <- "ATAC"
peak_counts <- GetAssayData(obj, slot = "counts")[peaks_with_motif, , drop = FALSE]

# per-cell raw intensity (sum of fragments in motif peaks)
motif_intensity <- Matrix::colSums(peak_counts)

# fraction of each cell’s ATAC counts that fall in motif peaks (size-adjusted)
motif_fraction <- motif_intensity / obj$nCount_ATAC

# add to metadata
obj$motif_id_current  <- as.numeric(motif_intensity)
obj$motif_id_current <- as.numeric(motif_fraction)
```


```{r}
# Following scripts are for better visualization with violin plots
```


```{r}
DefaultAssay(obj) <- "chromvar"

DimPlot(obj, reduction = "lsi", group.by = "seurat_clusters", dims = c(2, 3)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#279e68", "#d62728", "#aa40fc"))
FeaturePlot(obj, features = "MA1557.1", reduction = "lsi", dims = c(2,3), min.cutoff = "q10")  # size-adjusted
p <- VlnPlot(obj, features = "MA1557.1", group.by = "seurat_clusters", pt.size = 0)

custom_colors <- c("#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#279e68", "#d62728", "#aa40fc")
p + scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors)
```


```{r}
# Compute medians per cluster
feature <- "MA1557.1"
group_var <- "seurat_clusters"

# Extract data
data <- FetchData(obj, vars = c(feature, group_var))

# Compute median expression by cluster
medians <- tapply(data[[feature]], data[[group_var]], median)

# Optionally, create a color gradient based on median values
# e.g. low = blue, high = red
color_palette <- scales::col_numeric(
  palette = c("white", "red"),
  domain = range(medians)
)(medians)

# Match cluster order in plot
cluster_order <- levels(obj[[group_var]][,1])
cluster_colors <- setNames(color_palette[cluster_order], cluster_order)

# Make violin plot
p <- VlnPlot(
  obj,
  features = feature,
  group.by = group_var,
  pt.size = 0
)

# Apply colors manually
p + scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette)
```

```{r}

med_range <- range(medians)

# Make dummy data for the color bar
df_bar <- data.frame(x = seq(med_range[1], med_range[2], length.out = 100),
                     y = 1)

# Plot color bar
p2 <- ggplot(df_bar, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = x)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "white",
    high = "red",
    name = "Median Expression",
    limits = med_range
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_colorbar(
      title.position = "top",
      barwidth = unit(4, "in"),
      barheight = unit(0.3, "cm")
    )
  )
p + scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette) + p2
```

```{r}
pdf("SMAD5_motif.pdf", width = 6, height = 6)
p + scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette) + p2
dev.off()
```
